title: Token baseline probe - preloaded tool-definition cost across all emitting surfaces
executed_by: claude-code (headless); receipt reconstructed post-hoc from artifacts + logs
commandment_7: held - T.0 is a read-only probe and added no tests; the 4 failures are pre-existing and NOT caused by this leg (see drift D3)
check_verdict: check_token_baseline_fresh -> ok:true - 'preload_tokens_total: mcp_http=17310, mcp_stdio=13901, panel=14380, panel_worker=10264 [tiktoken/cl100k_base (proxy BPE, not Claude's tokenizer)] - surfaced, not judged'
method: tiktoken/cl100k_base - proxy BPE, explicitly NOT Claude's tokenizer, labelled as such in the artifact
re_rank_verdict: T.1 CONFIRMED, not demoted. spec-T's contract ('a small number demotes T.1 and that is the probe working') is satisfied in the other direction. Do not re-litigate.

drift.note: No receipt was written by the executing agent. The baton required harness/notes/receipts/T0.json at completion; the work landed and the return baton did not. This file is a post-hoc reconstruction, which is strictly weaker than one emitted at execution time.
drift.correction: Receipts must be a side effect of the RUNNER (run.ts), not an instruction to the agent. run.ts already knows commit before/after, exit codes, and files touched. An agent asked in prose for a JSON shape returns whatever it likes - observed twice this session.

drift.note: The mcp_stdio row is a Tool() shape RECONSTRUCTED from shared TOOL_DEFS, not the payload mcp_server.py::list_tools() actually emits. The artifact's own 'source' field says so honestly. list_tools() additionally appends 6 synapse_group_* tools, the inspector tool, and the scout tool - none in TOOL_DEFS, none counted in 13901.
drift.correction: stdio is under-reported by at least 8 tools. Re-measure by serialising list_tools() directly (import is side-effect-free, __main__ guard at mcp_server.py:1073) before the number is quoted externally.

drift.note: The suite was RED during T.0 (4 failed vs a 0-failure floor), all in tests/test_bridge_endpoint.py. Initially hypothesised as a Python 3.14 artifact. That hypothesis was WRONG.
drift.correction: Root cause was bridge_endpoint._pid_alive calling os.kill(pid, 0) on Windows, where CPython implements os.kill via TerminateProcess with the signal as exit code - it killed the process it probed, including the pytest runner at item 24. Platform-specific, not version-specific. Fixed in 0d78515; floor now 4716 passed / 0 failed.

drift.note: The authoring surface asserted in a governing document that mcp_stdio and mcp_http were byte-identical. It had measured the http surface twice and mislabelled one instance. Retracted in the baton before dispatch.
drift.correction: The evidence ladder needs a VERIFIED-DERIVED rung between STATIC and UNVERIFIED. A claim inferred from a probe is not a probe, and it wore a probe's label here.

drift.note: All suite runs used system Python 3.14.2, where the vendored cp311+cp313 tree is ABI-INACTIVE (warning emitted on every run). drop.json declares the supported runtime as H22 Python 3.13.10.
drift.correction: H22's bundled python313 cannot currently run the suite - 54 collection errors from missing test dependencies. Provisioning that environment is a prerequisite before the supported interpreter can be the canonical runner.

ratification_note: ratified:false is the correct resting state. The flip is human-only. This receipt records what happened; it does not sanction what happens next.
